strengths: Built and deployed production-grade agentic workflows at re:cinq using MCP frameworks, directly aligning with the need for tool-augmented, stateful agents beyond prompt chaining.

Proven track record in high-scale distributed systems — designed low-latency, real-time microservices at Dunnhumby handling millions of requests/sec, with deep expertise in event streaming, caching, and async I/O using DLang and Node.js.

Demonstrated engineering leadership through open-source platform governance (GISCollective), team mentoring, CI/CD infrastructure setup, and workshops fostering testing and system thinking — mirroring the need for culture-building and technical ownership.
concerns: No explicit evidence of fintech infrastructure experience (credit systems, regulatory compliance, or working-capital algorithms) — core domain knowledge is assumed but unverified.

Limited visibility into multi-agent architecture design at scale; current role mentions contribution to agent tooling but not end-to-end ownership of modular, auditable agent frameworks with feedback loops.
German language proficiency is listed as 'Basic,' which may hinder direct regulatory or operational communication in Berlin-based fintech contexts requiring local stakeholder alignment.
response.justification: Bogdan brings rare, hands-on production experience building agentic workflows and high-scale distributed systems — directly matching the core technical demands of the CTO role — while demonstrating leadership in engineering culture, observability, and tooling; his lack of explicit fintech background is a gap, but his proven ability to architect complex, real-time systems in regulated-like environments (e.g., high-throughput data pipelines) suggests strong transferable depth and adaptability.
